title: Check if the array is sorted

content.problemStatement:
Q2) A . Describe the step-by-step flow of a Python program's execution, starting from the source code
file (.py) to the final output on the screen ?   ---4 Marks.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N (the size of the array). The second line contains N space‑separated integers representing the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if the array is sorted in non‑decreasing order, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: First array is strictly increasing → sorted. Second array decreases at the first pair → not sorted. Third array is increasing → sorted.
Array [1,1] is non‑decreasing, so YES. Array [2,1] has 2>1, so NO.
The single test case is an already sorted sequence.

solution.approach: For each test case read the array sequentially. Keep the previous element (prev). While reading the next element (cur) compare prev and cur; if prev > cur the array is not sorted, set a flag to false. Continue reading the remaining elements to consume input. After the scan output "YES" if the flag stayed true, otherwise "NO". This runs in O(N) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, total O(\sum N) over all test cases.

code.Python3:
import sys
def main():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        N=int(next(it))
        prev=int(next(it)) if N>0 else None
        sorted_flag=True
        for _ in range(1,N):
            cur=int(next(it))
            if prev>cur:
                sorted_flag=False
            prev=cur
        out.append('YES' if sorted_flag else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
if __name__=='__main__':
    main()
